title: We just published a new experiment that might interest anyone following how AI is evolving in trading
author: blackbullmarkets
selftext:
Today, we tested five free versions of major AI platforms — Perplexity, Meta AI, Copilot, Ninja AI, and Mistral — to find out which one delivers the most consistent results when building and backtesting an Expert Advisor in MT5.

The goal was simple: to see which AI provides the most reliable edge without paying for a subscription.

Each AI was given the same task — create an EA for USD/JPY using the Ichimoku Cloud — with deliberately simple instructions to test how well each one understands trading logic and generates functional MQL5 code.

The results were fascinating. Some completed the task in under three seconds, others took more than forty. Only Ninja AI and Meta AI managed to run profitable backtests; the rest produced errors or failed to open trades. None of the free AIs were perfect, but this experiment shows that with enough testing and iteration, even free tools can be developed into something genuinely useful.

Has anyone else tried uploading their charts to AI for analysis? How accurate did you find the results?

Full video is on our YT channel if you’re interested!

comments.author: RuiZz9299
comments.body: I’ve been trying a few tools myself and the results really depend on how you use them. Recently I’ve been using Minara, which isn’t for building bots but helps make sense of what’s moving the market like funding and open interest. It’s been helpful for spotting setups and avoiding overtrading.
